message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (for a code change the named test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if reverted; for a doc/decision item, judge whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest). Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix H-018 in scripts/capture_transcripts.py
What: transcript scrubber redacts username in the project-dir slug (-Users-[REDACTED]-) at any length.
Test(s): test_transcript_scrub_redacts_username_in_project_dir_slug
Read the actual changed file(s); read the test; check no regression. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN. Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
011942b fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
1eb2f58 fix(hardening-p3): remediate 6 confirmation CONCERNs (2-agent pass)
2b07dfd fix(hardening-p3): batch 2 — security + observability seams (8 fixes, 1 FP)
1389242 harden/pass-1: 22 production-readiness fixes (assess→2-agent verify→fix→2-agent confirm) (#123)

30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
34	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
35	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
36	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
37	# for the full public-release consideration.
38	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
39	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED]    # The Claude project-dir SLUG (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc): redact the username segment
53	    # regardless of length (#H-018) — the bare-token rule below skips short handles to avoid
54	    # over-redacting common words, but the slug form is unambiguous PII at any length.
55	    re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+"),  # -Users-[REDACTED]- → -Users-[REDACTED]-
56	]
57	
58	
59	def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
60	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
61	
62	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
63	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
64	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
65	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
66	    """
67	    try:
68	        name = Path.home().name
69	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
70	        return None
71	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
72	        return None
73	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
74	
75	
76	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
77	
78	
79	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
80	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
81	        if pat.groups == 2:
82	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
83	        elif pat.groups == 1:
84	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
85	        else:
86	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
87	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
88	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
89	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
90	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
91	    return text
92	
93	
94	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
95	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
96	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
97	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
98	
99

content.content:
commit 011942ba0d024960245c8ab919fdf86388cff686
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 01:31:14 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
    
    Pass-5 confirmed findings (2-agent verified after self-refutation; revert-discriminated):
    
    Reliability / error contracts:
    - H-004 (med) malformed reasoning params (temperature/seed) raise the typed
      PermanentReasoningError instead of a raw ValueError/TypeError leaking past run()/run_json.
    - H-021 (low) QdrantVectorStore.query rejects non-positive k and skips returned points
      lacking our 'ref' payload (was an opaque KeyError).
    - H-022 (low) vectorstore int env vars parse via _int_env, naming the bad var instead of an
      opaque import-time "invalid literal for int()".
    - H-013 (low) the provenance-trail artifact tempfile is unlinked after the tracker copies it
      (was delete=False, leaked per tracked run).
    
    CI / supply-chain:
    - H-007 (med) license gate surfaces UNKNOWN/empty-license packages as a ::warning:: (hidden-
      copyleft vector) instead of passing them silently; still fails only on explicit deny matches.
    - H-008 (med) workflow pins GITHUB_TOKEN to least-privilege `permissions: contents: read`.
    - H-009 (med) the integration job verifies the Postgres/Qdrant service containers are
      reachable (pg_isready + /readyz) so the live tests can't silently SKIP and pass vacuously.
    
    Observability / governance:
    - H-011 (med) documented the deliberate recorder-optionality asymmetry between the graph gate
      node and run_study (the runner owns + always records to the trail; the node bridges).
    - H-017 (low) documented that serialized provenance records don't embed keying — the verifier
      supplies the secret out-of-band (key management; verify_records fails closed).
    
    Documentation honesty (assertion-vs-implementation):
    - H-010 (med) stable_seed: "distinct inputs get distinct seeds" corrected to the birthday
      bound of the truncated space (not a uniqueness guarantee).
    - H-012 (low) gatenode "atomically"/"terminal idempotency" claims scoped honestly.
    - H-023 (low) redact_creds "always scrubbed precisely" corrected to "precise when the conn
      string appears verbatim" (reformatted strings fall to the regex backstops).
    - H-018 (med) transcript scrubber redacts the username in the project-dir slug at any length.
    - H-024 (low) the in-process unfreeze test is marked illustrative; the seed-pinned subprocess
      test is the discriminating guard.
    
    Split-verdict (1-of-2) findings H-005/H-006/H-019/H-020 and rejected H-014 are documented in
    PASS-5.md as not-proceeding per the both-must-confirm protocol.
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 531 passed, 5 skipped.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git diff --stat && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts.py -q 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
Let me consider the regex character class for over-redaction risk. The pattern `(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/"']+` requires a literal `-Users-[REDACTED] or `-home-[REDACTED] prefix, which is the unambiguous slug form, not normal prose. The negated class stops at the next hyphen, whitespace, slash, or quote, so it captures exactly the username segment and preserves the path structure (`-hc-grc` remains). Ordering is correct too: it runs before the bare-username rule, and `_scrub` correctly handles the 1-group case (`\1[REDACTED]`).

One regression check: does this pattern wrongly match a real `/Users/[REDACTED] path that the prior `/Users/[REDACTED] rule already handled? No, it requires hyphens as delimiters, not slashes, so it only targets the Claude slug form. The two rules are complementary.

input.reasoning:
Fix is in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py (commit 011942b). It adds a regex to _SECRET_PATTERNS: re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+") with substitution \1[REDACTED] (correctly handled by _scrub's pat.groups==1 branch). This redacts the username segment of the Claude project-dir slug (-Users-[REDACTED]-) at ANY length, closing the gap where the bare-token rule (_username_pattern) skips handles shorter than 4 chars to avoid over-redacting common words.

APPROPRIATE: The pattern requires the literal hyphen-delimited prefix -Users-/-home- (the unambiguous slug form), so it cannot fire on ordinary prose or on normal /Users/ slash-paths (those are covered by the separate /(?:Users|home)/ rule — the two are complementary, no double-coverage conflict). The negated character class [^-\s/"']+ stops at the next hyphen/whitespace/slash/quote, so it masks exactly the username and preserves the legitimate path STRUCTURE (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc keeps -hc-grc). Idiomatic, consistent with the surrounding patterns, well-commented. Full tests/test_scripts.py passes (6 passed); no regression.

COMPLETE / DISCRIMINATING: test_transcript_scrub_redacts_username_in_project_dir_slug (tests/test_scripts.py:93) uses deliberately short names "tj" and "ci" — exactly the case the bare-token rule skips — so it isolates the new slug pattern. I verified discrimination by surgically removing only the new re.compile line: the test FAILED with AssertionError ("-Users-[REDACTED]-" still present). With the line restored it PASSES. Working tree restored clean (git diff empty).

content.text: Confirmed. The H-018 fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py` is both appropriate and complete, with a properly discriminating test.
